frameworks.name: reinforcement learning (RPE hypothesis)
frameworks.one_line: phasic dopamine encodes reward prediction errors in basal ganglia models

frameworks.name: state uncertainty / belief-state RL
frameworks.one_line: sensory feedback shapes state inference and thereby RPE dynamics

core_question: Why does dopamine ramp up before reward in some learned tasks, contrary to standard RPE convergence predictions?
key_assumptions: phasic dopamine maps onto RPE signals
sensory feedback informs the agent's current state estimate
manipulations: presence vs. gradual decrease of sensory feedback during a trial
brightness of virtual-navigation environment across conditions
key_findings: an unbiased learner with sensory feedback produces RPE ramps
with gradually decreasing feedback, dopamine forms a 'bump' rather than a flat ramp
ramp-up phase is larger under decreasing feedback than under sustained-high feedback
mice on a brightness-varying virtual navigation task showed both predicted patterns
interpretations: ramps and bumps are consistent with, not violations of, the RPE hypothesis once state uncertainty is included
sensory feedback reconciles previously conflicting dopamine ramp observations
